title: FP Markets Adds AI Signals And Sentiment Tools Through Acuity Deal - FinanceFeeds
body:
FP Markets has announced that it partnered with Acuity Trading to introduce a suite of artificial intelligence-based trading tools, as brokers continue to expand analytics capabilities inside their platforms to retain active traders.

The integration gives FP Markets clients access to a new Intelligence suite that combines sentiment analysis, trading signals, and real-time market data within the broker's existing infrastructure. The rollout reflects a wider shift across the CFD and forex industry, where brokers compete not only on spreads and execution but also on the quality of embedded research tools.

The Intelligence suite is available through the FP Markets client portal and connects directly with MetaTrader 4 and MetaTrader 5, allowing traders to access signals and analytics within familiar trading environments. This reduces the need for external tools, a common friction point for retail and semi-professional traders.

The offering includes AnalysisIQ, which provides trading signals based on market data, and Assets Overview, which aggregates insights across multiple asset classes. NewsIQ delivers real-time updates and research, linking price movements to underlying developments.

Additional tools such as a calendar and research terminal allow users to filter information based on preferences and trading strategies. The structure reflects a move toward centralized dashboards where multiple data streams are combined into a single interface.

The partnership highlights how brokers are responding to increasing demand for faster and more structured information. Traders now expect platforms to provide not only execution but also context, including sentiment indicators, news interpretation, and actionable signals.

Artificial intelligence plays a growing role in this process. By processing large volumes of market data, AI systems can identify patterns, summarize developments, and generate signals that would be difficult to produce manually in real time. This allows traders to react more quickly, particularly in short-term strategies where timing affects performance.

At the same time, the expansion of AI tools raises questions about differentiation. Many brokers now offer similar features, including sentiment indicators and signal generation. The challenge lies in how these tools are integrated into workflows and whether they provide consistent value rather than noise.

For FP Markets, the integration with Acuity allows the firm to add external analytics without building the infrastructure internally. For Acuity, the partnership expands distribution by embedding its data and models directly into a brokerage environment.

The CFD and forex brokerage sector has moved toward platform consolidation, where research, analytics, and execution operate within the same system. Traders increasingly prefer environments where they can identify opportunities and execute trades without switching between multiple applications.

This trend has intensified competition among brokers. Pricing and execution remain relevant, but they are no longer sufficient on their own. Platforms must also provide tools that help users interpret markets and make decisions more efficiently.

Aaron Hill, Chief Market Analyst at FP Markets, commented, "Our mission is to equip clients with resources needed to navigate an evolving trading environment. We have teamed up with Acuity Trading to offer a suite of trading tools that support analysis, opportunity identification, and strategy development."

Andrew Lane, CEO at Acuity Trading, commented, "By bringing sentiment, signals, and real-time market insight into FP Markets Intelligence, we are helping traders access information inside their workflow and respond to market conditions with greater clarity."

The emphasis on workflow integration reflects a key battleground in the industry. Tools that sit outside the trading process often go unused. Embedding analytics directly into trading platforms increases the likelihood that traders will rely on them.

The addition of AI-driven tools provides traders with faster access to structured information, but it does not remove the need for judgment. Signals and sentiment indicators can support decision-making, but they depend on underlying models that may not perform consistently across all market conditions.

For platforms, the focus is shifting toward combining multiple layers of functionality. Execution, analytics, and research are increasingly delivered together, reducing the need for third-party tools and keeping users within a single ecosystem.

The partnership between FP Markets and Acuity fits into that direction. It adds another layer of analytics to the broker's offering while aligning with broader industry trends toward integrated trading environments.

The next phase will depend on adoption and performance. Traders will assess whether the tools provide useful signals and whether they improve outcomes over time. Brokers, in turn, will continue to refine how these features are presented and how they interact with execution systems.

title: FP Markets Integrates Acuity Trading's AI Signal Platform for Retail Clients
body:
The deal gives FP Markets clients access to market analysis tools across thousands of instruments via MT4, MT5, and a dedicated client portal.

FP Markets has teamed up with Acuity Trading to provide its clients with AI-driven trading signals and market research tools, the broker announced today (Monday). The collaboration introduces FP Markets Intelligence, a multi-tool suite accessible through the broker's Client Portal that the company says covers sentiment analysis, market signals, and real-time news data across a broad range of instruments.

The FP Markets Intelligence suite integrates directly with MetaTrader 4 and MetaTrader 5, as well as the broker's own Client Portal. The offering includes three core components, according to FP Markets: AnalysisIQ, which produces market signals; Assets Overview, which delivers data across multiple asset classes; and NewsIQ, which aggregates real-time research and market updates. A Research Terminal and economic Calendar round out the package, the company said.

"FP Markets shares our belief that traders make better decisions when intelligence is delivered clearly, in context, and directly inside the trading workflow," Andrew Lane, CEO of Acuity Trading, said.

"By bringing Acuity's AI-powered sentiment, signals and real-time market insight into FP Markets Intelligence, we're helping traders cut through the noise, stay aligned with market conditions, and act with greater confidence."

FP Markets describes the platform as designed to help traders filter market insights based on individual preferences and refine their approaches accordingly. The company has not disclosed the precise number of instruments covered, characterizing the scope broadly as "thousands."

The deal builds on a string of technology moves by the Sydney-based broker in recent years. FP Markets previously partnered with trading signals provider xsee, and the Acuity integration now extends that push into a more consolidated AI-powered product.

title: Exclusive: FXTM to Give Up FCA Licence, Raises UAE Bet
body:
It is upgrading its UAE Cat 5 licence to Cat 1 and partnering with a local Indonesian broker.

FXTM, the forex and contracts for difference (CFD) broker brand owned by Andrey Dashin, has decided to give up its Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) licence in the United Kingdom to focus more on Asian and Middle Eastern markets, FinanceMagnates.com has learned. Indeed, the broker is now gaining full brokerage status in the UAE and has also partnered with a local Indonesian broker.

Singapore Summit: Meet the largest APAC brokers you know (and those you still don't!)

The brokerage giant labelled its move out of the UK as "part of its global realignment of strategic priorities." It has concluded that the UK retail market is no longer its core growth focus area.

At the same time, it believes that there is a significant long-term opportunity in the Middle East and Asia.

FinanceMagnates.com earlier reported that Exinity, which is the entity behind FXTM, secured a Category 5 licence from Dubai's Capital Markets Authority. The Category 5 licence only allows companies to promote and market financial products in the UAE, but they must onboard clients and execute trades through an offshore entity.

Now the broker will upgrade its Category 5 licence to a Category 1 licence, with which it can operate as a full broker within the UAE. That means it can take in clients, accept funds, and execute trades locally.

Although many brokers have flocked to the UAE in recent years, only a handful have obtained the full Category 1 licence. Plus500, XTB, and RoboMarkets are among the few names that have become full brokers in the UAE.

FXTM is also advancing its Asia expansion strategy through a planned strategic partnership with a fully licensed Indonesian brokerage firm, operating as Invetra (PT INVETRA TEKNOLOGI BERJANGKA), which is regulated by BAPPEBTI.

Under this collaboration, Invetra will offer locally regulated trading powered by FXTM's proprietary multi-asset technology stack. This model allows Indonesian clients to benefit from global standard tools and execution capabilities while remaining fully compliant with local frameworks.

Indonesia has also attracted many foreign brokerage brands. Plus500 bought a local broker, while Doo Group and XTB also gained a licence there.

Meanwhile, FXTM is not the only broker to exit the UK market. HTFX, AETOS, and GMI Markets are a few recent ones to give up their FCA authorisation. While some completely wrapped up their business, others are narrowing down their focus markets.

Interestingly, FXTM also stopped offering services to retail clients under its Cyprus entity in February 2021 and gave up that licence later in 2023.

title: HOOD Stock Rises on Trump Account Partnership as Robinhood Expands Its Financial Empire - TokenPost
body:
Robinhood (HOOD) edged higher Tuesday as broader equity markets rallied and the Trump administration announced the fintech platform as its chosen brokerage for the newly launched Trump accounts program. Shares were trading around $70, recovering modestly from a year-to-date low of $63.70.

The White House has tapped Robinhood and BNY Mellon to jointly administer Trump accounts, a federally backed savings initiative targeting children born between January 1, 2025, and the end of 2028. Under the program, the government seeds each qualifying account with $1,000. BNY Mellon will oversee account management and support app development, while Robinhood serves as the primary brokerage and initial trustee -- a role analysts say could generate significant incremental revenue over time.

The initiative, established through the Big Beautiful Bill, has already attracted major private backers. Michael Dell pledged $6.25 billion, while firms including JPMorgan Chase, Mastercard, SoFi, and BNY Mellon have all signed on to the program.

The announcement adds another growth driver to a company already expanding aggressively. Robinhood has moved into tokenized equities across European markets, launched its Ventures program on the New York Stock Exchange to offer retail exposure to private firms like Stripe and Revolut, and deepened its crypto footprint through the acquisition of exchange Bitstamp. A new banking product, developed in partnership with Coastal Community Bank, rounds out its diversification push. Robinhood's annual revenue has surged from $256 million in 2021 to over $1.5 billion, and the stock earned a place in the S&P 500 in 2025.

From a technical standpoint, HOOD is forming a falling wedge pattern with a declining ADX reading of 34, down from 42 in February -- signals that selling pressure may be exhausting. A bullish breakout could target $84, approximately 22% above current levels, while a close below $63.70 would invalidate the setup.

title: Robinhood Joins Trump Accounts Program as Treasury-Backed Brokerage Partner - TokenPost
body:
Robinhood is entering a significant new chapter by partnering with the U.S. Department of the Treasury as part of the newly launched Trump Accounts program. Under this initiative, Robinhood will serve as both the designated brokerage provider and initial trustee, while BNY Mellon takes on the role of financial agent responsible for managing account infrastructure and oversight.

The program introduces a secure, white-label application developed in collaboration with the National Design Studio. Designed with first-time users in mind, the platform offers families a structured and accessible way to manage their government-backed financial accounts. Despite Robinhood's involvement in the front-end experience, the Treasury Department retains full operational authority over the system, with strict compliance requirements and public fund safeguards built into the framework.

This government partnership follows Robinhood's recent announcement of a $1.5 billion share buyback program, set to launch in Q1 2026 and spanning three years, signaling strong confidence in its long-term financial position. Markets responded positively to the Treasury news, pushing Robinhood shares up 1.92% to $70.22, reflecting renewed investor interest and active accumulation despite intraday volatility.

However, not all developments are favorable. Robinhood faces a notable setback in its pursuit of a role in the highly anticipated SpaceX IPO. Reports suggest that Morgan Stanley's E*TRADE platform has emerged as the frontrunner for handling retail share distribution, bolstered by Morgan Stanley's existing position as the IPO's lead underwriter. While SpaceX is reportedly planning to allocate up to 30% of IPO shares to retail investors, a higher-than-usual figure, Robinhood's participation may be limited or reduced, potentially impacting its visibility in one of the most watched public offerings in recent memory.

Robinhood continues to diversify aggressively, balancing government-sector expansion with competitive pressures in the retail investing space.

title: Treasury's Trump Accounts: A Robinhood Customer Acquisition Program In Disguise
body:
OBSERVATIONS FROM THE FINTECH SNARK TANK

The Treasury Department made it official: Bank of New York Mellon will serve as financial agent for Trump Accounts, and Robinhood will be the brokerage and initial trustee.

Together, they'll build a white-label app launching July 4th, giving every eligible American child a $1,000 government-seeded, tax-deferred investment account.

Expect BNY to talk about "expanding access to financial opportunity for all Americans" and for Robinhood to feed us their "democratizing finance" line.

Now let me tell you what this actually is: a customer acquisition program for Robinhood and a kick in the teeth to community financial institutions.

The Math Behind the Trump Accounts

As of March 31st, more than 4 million children had already been signed up for Trump Accounts, with over 1 million eligible for the $1,000 pilot contribution.

That's 4 million families now being onboarded onto a Robinhood-powered platform. Families that will contribute up to $5,000 per year, with employers permitted to add another $2,500 annually.

The fig leaf Treasury is offering other banks: users will be able to roll over the accounts to other financial institutions within a year.

The overwhelming majority of those 4 million families won't roll those accounts over anywhere. Robinhood knows it, BNY knows it, and the Treasury can't be that clueless to not know it, as well.

Robinhood just got a taxpayer-subsidized pipeline to 4 million households with children -- households they will market to, cross-sell to, and retain for the next 18 years until those kids can withdraw their funds.

That's not "democratizing finance," it's a government-funded customer acquisition engine for a publicly traded fintech.

Community Financial Institutions: "You Don't Matter"

Let me ask a simple question the Treasury apparently never asked: why wasn't any portion of this program structured to flow through community banks and credit unions?

Community-based financial institutions have been the bedrock of family savings relationships in this country for generations. They're embedded in their communities. They have the trust relationships.

In addition, many of them already offer custodial accounts and youth savings programs. For decades, the first bank account a kid ever had was at the local community bank or credit union.

Treasury just decided that none of that matters. Instead, they handed the keys to a $130 billion custodian-for-hire and a trading app that built its brand on making investing feel like a slot machine.

No competitive bidding process was disclosed. There's no community bank participation tier or credit union partnership pathway. The financial details of the arrangement weren't even disclosed, so we don't know what BNY and Robinhood are getting paid.

What we do know is that community banks and credit unions are getting nothing.

If the goal was really financial inclusion and access, a network of 4,500 community banks and 4,800 credit unions already covers virtually every zip code in America. Treasury chose to ignore all of them.

Why Robinhood?

Robinhood is a legitimate, well-capitalized company with scale and a good app. But the decision to make it the trustee for children's investment accounts funded by the federal government deserves some scrutiny.

But this is the same company that halted trading in GameStop and AMC in January 2021, triggering congressional hearings and a $70 million FINRA fine, the largest in the regulator's history at the time.

It's a company whose core business model was built on payment for order flow, a practice that regulators have questioned for years on the grounds that it creates conflicts of interest at the expense of retail investors.

Now they're the initial trustee for an account that children cannot withdraw from until they turn 18. That's a long time horizon to hope nothing goes wrong with the platform, the company, or its regulatory standing.

Here's the part that should raise eyebrows: BNY was among the employers that pledged to match the Treasury's $1,000 seed contribution for children of U.S. employees. And then BNY was selected as the financial agent for the program.

I'm not saying that's a quid pro quo. I'm saying if a community bank CEO made a $1,000 pledge to a federal program and then won the contract to run it, someone would be asking questions.

The Rollover Window Is a Red Herring

Treasury is pointing to the one-year rollover provision as evidence that this isn't a monopoly arrangement. Don't be fooled.

Financial services is a sticky business. Switching costs are low in theory and high in practice. People don't switch banks when they're unhappy; they certainly don't switch investment custodians managing their kid's government account when they're indifferent.

The families who enroll, connect their bank accounts, and set up contributions through the Robinhood-powered app are not moving. The rollover window is a talking point, not a structural protection for competition.

By July 5th, Robinhood will have more custodial accounts than most community banks have total customers.

The Bottom Line on Trump Accounts

Trump Accounts might be good policy. There's a reasonable argument for seeding investment accounts for children, building a culture of long-term investing, and nudging families toward equity markets.

The execution here reflects a fundamental blindness to the community banking ecosystem, and a concerning willingness to hand a massive government-backed advantage to a company that has a checkered regulatory history and an obvious commercial interest in keeping these families on its platform forever.

The Treasury had an opportunity to design this program in a way that strengthened local financial institutions, deepened community relationships, and distributed the economic benefit broadly. Instead, they handed the contract to a Wall Street custodian and a trading app, called it "democratization," and issued a press release.

Community banks and credit unions got a front-row seat to watch their next generation of customers get onboarded somewhere else -- courtesy of the US government.

title: Treasury Taps BNY Mellon and Robinhood for Trump Accounts Program Serving Millions of Newborns
body:
The U.S. Treasury Department has designated Bank of New York Mellon ($BK) as a financial agent to support implementation of the new Trump Accounts program, with Robinhood ($HOOD) serving as brokerage and initial trustee. The program, introduced earlier this year, provides government-backed investment accounts for U.S. newborns, including a $1,000 federal contribution for eligible children born between 2025 and 2028.

* BNY will manage initial accounts and help develop a Treasury-controlled mobile app for account access.

* Robinhood will provide brokerage services and act as initial trustee for the accounts.

* The Treasury estimates roughly 25 million families will be eligible for the program.

* Approximately 4 million children have already been signed up, with about 25% eligible for the $1,000 deposit.

* Accounts must be invested in low-cost U.S. stock index funds until beneficiaries reach age 18.

* Funds grow tax-deferred and transition to IRA-style tax treatment when the child turns 18.

* Participants will be able to roll over accounts to other financial institutions after an initial period.

* Bank of New York Mellon has lobbied on legislation and regulation affecting custody banking and tax treatment of financial services, according to federal disclosures.

* Robinhood has lobbied on issues related to brokerage regulation and retail investor access, according to federal disclosures.

Relevant Companies

* BK - Selected as Treasury's financial agent, responsible for managing accounts and infrastructure.

* HOOD - Serving as brokerage and trustee, providing investment access and platform integration.
